title: Fix synced usage pricing
description: Blog/session usage rows currently sync with missing or stale cost_usd values because pricing is not computed consistently for provider/model pairs. Add provider-aware pricing for known OpenAI and Anthropic models, including Codex auto-review per OpenAI docs, and cover it with regression tests.

title: Align blog usage sync with blog ingest contract
description: Update the macOS blog usage sync to match the blog repository ingest contract for Claude, Codex, and OpenCode Go: preserve opencode as the agent, carry opencode-go as provider, parse OpenCode nested timestamps, update coverage tests, and clarify settings copy.
notes:
Implementation complete: aligned blog usage sync with the blog ingest contract. OpenCode remains agent=opencode, providerID such as opencode-go is preserved, nested OpenCode time.created timestamps are parsed, combined source coverage was added, and settings copy now names Claude/Codex/OpenCode Go. Validation: macOS app build succeeds. Targeted test execution remains blocked by pre-existing OutageTrackingTests compile errors tracked separately in ClaudeMeter-6rc. Attempted bd close but bd failed with: column depends_on_id could not be found in any table in scope.
Closed via bd update --status closed because bd close fails in embedded mode dependency blocker check: column depends_on_id could not be found in any table in scope.

title: ClaudeMeterTests target broken: OutageTrackingTests references nonexistent members
description: OutageTrackingTests.swift references UsageViewModel.isOutageError/activeClaudeIncident/isClaudeServiceDown and MockAPIService.setMockError/setMockSnapshot, MockCredentialProvider.configure — none defined in the repo. Pre-existing on main (verified via clean-tree build-for-testing). Blocks running the entire test suite. Likely an outage-tracking feature whose impl/mocks were never merged (or the test was left behind).

title: Drop ccusage backend; make native JSONL parser the sole token-usage source
description: ccusage numbers disagree with the native parser (unscoped/all-agents + LiteLLM pricing vs Claude-only + hardcoded Anthropic pricing). Remove CCUsageRunnerService + tests + wiring and the now-dead multi-agent plumbing (UsageProvider, ProviderUsageSummary, TokenUsageDataSource, snapshot provider fields). Delete scripts/compare_usage.swift. Blog sync unaffected (independent parser).
notes: Done: deleted CCUsageRunnerService.swift + tests + scripts/compare_usage.swift; removed ccusage wiring from UsageViewModel & DependencyContainer; removed dead multi-agent plumbing (UsageProvider, ProviderUsageSummary, TokenUsageDataSource, snapshot provider fields) from TokenUsage.swift. macOS app BUILD SUCCEEDED. Test suite could NOT run due to pre-existing OutageTrackingTests breakage (filed separately) — unrelated to this change. Not committed (conservative profile).

title: Correct 0.11.1 release notes
description: Replace the incorrect archive wording in the 0.11.1 changelog and live GitHub release body with the intended maintenance-release note for release metadata and Sparkle appcast notes.

title: Clean up historical release metadata
description: Restore missing changelog and appcast release-note metadata, recover available GitHub release assets from duplicate drafts, and verify the release inventory remains consistent.
notes: Completed release metadata cleanup: restored changelog 0.10.8, repaired recoverable release assets, removed duplicate drafts, and fixed v0.11.0 release/appcast notes.

title: Add unscoped ccusage backend
description: Implement a macOS-only unscoped ccusage local usage backend with pnx/pnpx/pnpm dlx runner fallback, preserving provider identity for future multi-agent UI while keeping native Claude parsing as fallback.

title: Reduce blog usage sync throttle
description: The passive blog usage sync currently waits about one hour between attempts. Reduce the throttle aggressively while keeping manual sync bypass behavior and tests aligned.

title: Add passive blog usage sync
description: Implement macOS-only passive sync from local Claude, Codex, and OpenCode usage sources into the blog usage ingest endpoint, with Keychain token storage, Settings controls, hourly throttle, and tests.
